# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: compute-engine
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: n1-standard-1
    disks:
    - autoDelete: true
      boot: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
        diskSizeGb: 10
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
      accessConfigs:
      - name: External NAT
        type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
- name: cloud-sql-instance
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
  properties:
    region: us-central1
    settings:
      tier: db-n1-standard-1
      ipConfiguration: IPV4_INTERNAL
      dataDiskSizeGb: 10
      activationPolicy: ALWAYS
      authorizedNetworks:
        networks:
          - name: default
            value: 0.0.0.0/0
- name: cloud-storage
  type: storage.v1beta3.bucket
  properties:
    location: us-central1
    storageClass: STANDARD
    versioning:
      enabled: true
- name: load-balancer
  type: compute.v1.instanceGroupManager
  properties:
    baseInstanceName: lb
    instanceTemplate: global/instanceTemplates/compute-engine
    targetSize: 2
    namedPorts:
    - name: http
      port: 80
